title: FairTradeWorker
summary: Two-sided construction marketplace with QuickBooks-native payments and AI-powered estimation.
description: Two-sided construction marketplace with QuickBooks-native payments and AI-powered estimation.
fullDescription: FairTradeWorker is a full-stack construction marketplace connecting homeowners with vetted contractors. Homeowners post jobs, contractors bid competitively, and the platform handles payments through QuickBooks Online integration. Built across multiple repos — Next.js frontend, a Java/Spring Boot backend (ftw-svc) with STOMP WebSocket realtime, and a React Native mobile app.
challenge: Building a marketplace that handles the full lifecycle — from job posting through bidding, contract signing, milestone-based payments, and dispute resolution — while keeping contractors honest and homeowners protected.
solution: Three-tier architecture: homeowner posts job, contractor bids, subcontractor handles specialty work. QuickBooks handles all payment flows natively. ConstructionAI powers instant cost estimates so homeowners know what's fair before a single bid comes in.
features: QuickBooks-native invoicing and payout
AI-powered cost estimation via ConstructionAI
Real-time bidding with STOMP WebSocket
Three-role system: homeowner, contractor, subcontractor
Mobile app with 30+ screens (React Native/Expo)
6 revenue streams including white-label licensing
seoTitle: FairTradeWorker — Construction Marketplace with QuickBooks Payments
seoDescription: A two-sided construction marketplace built by Walt Burge: QuickBooks-native payments, real-time bidding, and AI cost estimation across web and mobile.

title: MsHomePros
summary: Contractor business platform — estimation, proposals, tracking, and payments powered by ConstructionAI.
description: Contractor business platform — estimation, proposals, tracking, and payments powered by ConstructionAI.
fullDescription: MsHomePros is a contractor-facing business management platform. Contractors generate AI-powered estimates, create professional proposals, track jobs, and manage client relationships. The estimation engine runs on a custom fine-tuned LLM deployed on RunPod Serverless.
challenge: Construction contractors still price jobs on gut feel and napkin math. Getting accurate, professional estimates out the door fast is the difference between winning and losing bids.
solution: ConstructionAI generates line-item estimates in seconds. The platform wraps that in professional PDF proposals, job tracking, and client management — everything a contractor needs to run their business from one place.
features: AI-powered line-item estimation
Professional PDF proposal generation
Job tracking and scheduling
Client relationship management
Real contractor in production (MHP Construction, Oxford MS)
seoTitle: MsHomePros — AI Estimation & Proposals for Contractors
seoDescription: A contractor business platform built by Walt Burge: AI line-item estimating, professional proposals, and job tracking, running in production for a real Oxford, MS contractor.

title: ConstructionAI
summary: Fine-tuned Llama 3.1 8B for construction cost estimation — 18,000+ training examples, deployed on RunPod.
description: Fine-tuned Llama 3.1 8B for construction cost estimation — 18,000+ training examples, deployed on RunPod.
fullDescription: ConstructionAI is a custom fine-tuned large language model built specifically for construction cost estimation. Trained on 18,000+ curated examples covering residential and commercial trades, it generates detailed line-item estimates with material quantities, labor hours, and market-adjusted pricing. Currently scaling to 500K+ training examples via synthetic data distillation.
challenge: No existing AI model understands construction pricing at the line-item level. Generic LLMs hallucinate costs and miss trade-specific nuances like regional labor rates, material waste factors, and code requirements.
solution: Built a full training pipeline: curated real-world estimation data, generated synthetic examples via distillation from larger models, fine-tuned Llama 3.1 8B with custom hyperparameters, and deployed on RunPod Serverless at ~$0.002 per estimate.
features: Line-item cost breakdowns by trade
Material quantity and waste calculations
Regional labor rate adjustment
RunPod Serverless deployment (~$0.002/estimate)
8 specialized tool functions
Scaling to 500K+ training examples (v5 pipeline)
seoTitle: ConstructionAI — A Fine-Tuned LLM for Cost Estimation
seoDescription: A custom fine-tuned Llama model for construction cost estimation by Walt Burge — 18,000+ training examples, line-item breakdowns, deployed serverless at ~$0.002 per estimate.

title: FTW Mobile
summary: The FairTradeWorker marketplace as a native app — three roles, 30+ screens, real-time bidding on the phone.
description: The FairTradeWorker marketplace as a native app — three roles, 30+ screens, real-time bidding on the phone.
fullDescription: FTW Mobile is the React Native / Expo app for the FairTradeWorker marketplace, built mobile-first because that is where contractors and homeowners actually live. It carries the full marketplace loop — post a job, receive and review bids, message, and pay — across three distinct role experiences, with STOMP WebSocket realtime so bids and messages land instantly.
challenge: Contractors run their business from a truck, not a desk. The marketplace had to feel native and fast on a phone, with three genuinely different role experiences sharing one codebase.
solution: A single Expo codebase with role-aware navigation for homeowner, contractor, and subcontractor. STOMP/SockJS over WebSocket keeps bids and chat realtime against the same Spring Boot backend as web, so there is one source of truth.
features: Three role experiences in one Expo codebase
30+ screens covering the full marketplace loop
STOMP WebSocket realtime bidding and messaging
Shared backend with the web app (ftw-svc)
ConstructionAI estimates in-app
Built for App Store release
seoTitle: FTW Mobile — The Construction Marketplace, Native
seoDescription: The FairTradeWorker marketplace as a React Native app by Walt Burge: three role experiences, 30+ screens, and real-time bidding over WebSocket.

title: AEON
summary: A formal-verification engine — 73 analysis engines that prove code correct and hunt vulnerabilities before it ships.
description: A formal-verification engine — 73 analysis engines that prove code correct and hunt vulnerabilities before it ships.
fullDescription: AEON is a formal-verification and code-analysis platform that runs a portfolio of 73 engines — 22 of them focused on cybersecurity — across a codebase to catch correctness bugs, security holes, and unsafe patterns before they reach production. It runs as both a CLI and a layer inside the W.A.L.T. platform, scanning real projects like FairTradeWorker and MsHomePros.
challenge: Most AI-written code looks right and passes a glance. Catching the bugs that only show up under adversarial input — capability leaks, missing auth boundaries, injection — needs more than a linter.
solution: A 73-engine analysis portfolio that combines correctness checks with an OWASP-aligned security suite, runnable from the CLI or wired into an automated pipeline so every project gets the same adversarial pass.
features: 73 analysis engines (22 cybersecurity)
OWASP-aligned vulnerability detection
Portfolio scans across multiple real projects
CLI plus pipeline integration
Correctness and safety in one pass
seoDescription: A 73-engine formal-verification and security-analysis platform by Walt Burge that proves code correct and hunts vulnerabilities before it ships.

title: Forge — Model Trainer
summary: A desktop app for fine-tuning custom models on rented GPUs — launch a training run without touching a terminal.
description: A desktop app for fine-tuning custom models on rented GPUs — launch a training run without touching a terminal.
fullDescription: Forge is the Strata Model Trainer: an Electron + Next.js desktop application that turns fine-tuning a custom LLM into a guided workflow. It provisions GPUs on vast.ai, manages datasets and hyperparameters, launches and monitors training runs, and pulls the finished model back — the toolchain behind ConstructionAI, packaged so it can be repeated for any model.
challenge: Fine-tuning a model usually means a tangle of SSH sessions, rented GPUs, and shell scripts that only the person who wrote them can run.
solution: Wrap the whole loop — GPU provisioning, dataset prep, hyperparameters, launch, and monitoring — in a desktop GUI on top of vast.ai, so a training run is a few clicks instead of a terminal marathon.
features: One-click GPU provisioning on vast.ai
Dataset and hyperparameter management
Launch and monitor training runs from a GUI
Electron + Next.js desktop app
The pipeline behind ConstructionAI, productized
seoTitle: Forge — A Desktop Trainer for Custom AI Models
seoDescription: An Electron desktop app by Walt Burge for fine-tuning custom LLMs on rented GPUs — provision, train, and monitor without touching a terminal.

title: Engram
summary: Open-source screen memory — your computer reads and remembers everything on your screen, searchable forever, and nothing ever leaves your machine.
description: Open-source screen memory — your computer reads and remembers everything on your screen, searchable forever, and nothing ever leaves your machine.
fullDescription: Engram gives any computer a photographic memory. It reads the text off your screen with on-device OCR, redacts secrets, and stores it in a local searchable index — so you, or any AI agent, can search everything you have ever seen. One pip install, MIT licensed, and not a single pixel leaves your machine. It is the open-source core of the perception layer behind W.A.L.T.
challenge: Microsoft promised this with Recall and pulled it; Rewind.ai built it and walked away. The same thing killed both: they sent your screen to the cloud. Nobody wants their entire screen history sitting on someone else's server.
solution: Keep all of it local. Capture runs in-process, OCR and secret-redaction happen on-device, and everything lands in a local SQLite full-text index exposed over a REST API and an MCP server — so any AI agent can query your memory without a single network call.
features: One-command install: pip install engram-memory
Reads the text off your screen — stores no images
Secret redaction before anything is written
Full-text search across everything you have seen
REST API + MCP server so any AI agent can use it
MIT licensed, 100% local — nothing leaves your machine
seoDescription: An MIT-licensed screen-memory tool by Walt Burge: on-device OCR, secret redaction, full-text search, and an MCP server — 100% local, nothing leaves your machine.

title: Tessera
summary: A markdown-native programming language for AI agents — write agents in .t.md files and verify them formally before they run.
description: A markdown-native programming language for AI agents — write agents in .t.md files, verify them formally before they run, and execute them on real LLM, LangChain, and PyTorch backends.
fullDescription: Tessera is a programming language where the markdown file IS the program. Each .t.md file is simultaneously a runnable agent, a browsable Obsidian note, and a formally-verifiable safety boundary. Agents are written as substrate-tagged code fences — logic, agent, memory, prompt, tool, neural — and the compiler enforces the boundaries between them. It lives at the center of a five-system ecosystem: AEON verifies the agent, Synapse persists its memory, Obsidian is where it's written, and Ollama/Anthropic/LangChain/PyTorch do the heavy lifting.
challenge: Building an AI agent shouldn't require five frameworks, three vendor SDKs, and a vector database to babysit. Agent behavior ends up scattered across files and prompt strings nobody can read or verify — and there's no way to prove an agent is safe before you run it in production.
solution: Make markdown the substrate. A .t.md file compiles to a Substrate IR that AEON's 73 engines verify the same way they verify Python or Rust — catching capability leaks and PII egress before the agent ever runs. Substrate fences declare named modes of thinking; capability grants in frontmatter propagate to every region and are enforced at compile, spawn, and runtime.
features: Write agents in .t.md files — markdown is the source of truth
9 shipped substrates: logic, agent, 4 memory kinds, prompt, tool, neural
Formal verification via AEON before the agent runs (73 engines)
Capability gating enforced at compile, spawn, and runtime
Synapse-backed semantic memory + Obsidian vault discovery
Cognitive traits — installable reasoning postures as first-class code
seoTitle: Tessera — A Markdown-Native Language for AI Agents
seoDescription: A programming language by Walt Burge where the markdown file is the agent — write in .t.md, verify formally with AEON before it runs, execute on real LLM backends.

title: W.A.L.T.
summary: Distributed AI platform spanning three machines — 20 AGI layers, formal verification, screen vision, and autonomous execution.
description: Distributed AI platform spanning three machines — 20 AGI layers, 73-engine verification, screen vision, and autonomous execution.
fullDescription: W.A.L.T. is a unified AI platform that turns Claude Code into something closer to a full operating system. It runs across three networked machines (Mac, Mini PC, GPU workstation) and includes screen understanding (Claude Eyes — 64 MCP tools), formal code verification (AEON — 73 engines), autonomous overnight execution, persistent goal tracking, an immune system for self-healing, and a file-backed event bus (Nerve) connecting 20 AGI-inspired layers.
challenge: AI assistants are stateless and reactive. They forget everything between sessions, can't see your screen, can't verify their own code, and can't work while you sleep.
solution: Built persistence, perception, and autonomy from scratch. Nerve connects all subsystems via pub/sub events. Eyes reads the screen. AEON verifies code formally. The overnight runner executes multi-project work queues autonomously. Goals persist across sessions with velocity tracking.
features: Claude Eyes: 64 MCP tools for screen + webcam understanding
AEON: 73-engine formal verification (22 cybersecurity)
Nerve: file-backed pub/sub event bus across all layers
Overnight runner: autonomous multi-project execution
20 AGI layers: goals, immune, memory, metacognition, and more
3-node distributed network (Mac + Mini PC + GPU workstation)
seoTitle: W.A.L.T. — A Distributed AI Operating Layer
seoDescription: A three-machine AI platform by Walt Burge: 20 AGI-inspired layers, screen understanding, 73-engine verification, and autonomous overnight execution.

title: Twin Brain (Vault MCP)
summary: A persistent memory layer for AI — an MCP server that gives an assistant a brain that survives across every session.
description: A persistent memory layer for AI — an MCP server that gives an assistant a brain that survives across every session.
fullDescription: Twin Brain is the persistence layer behind W.A.L.T.: an MCP server with 13 tools that lets an AI assistant capture decisions, recall prior context, and link ideas across sessions, backed by a semantic embedding index over a markdown knowledge vault. It turns a stateless assistant into one that remembers what you decided last week and surfaces it before you ask.
challenge: Every AI session starts from zero. Context, decisions, and hard-won reasoning evaporate the moment the window closes.
solution: A markdown-native knowledge vault wrapped in an MCP server: capture zettels, decisions, and open loops as files; index them semantically; and recall the relevant ones automatically at the start of the next conversation.
features: MCP server with 13 memory tools
Semantic embedding index over a markdown vault
Capture decisions, zettels, and open loops as files
Automatic recall of relevant prior context
Lifecycle hooks that sync memory across sessions
seoTitle: Twin Brain — Persistent Memory for AI Agents
seoDescription: An MCP server by Walt Burge that gives an AI assistant a persistent brain: capture decisions, semantic recall, and context that survives across every session.

title: MHP Construction
summary: A real Oxford, MS contractor running on a custom AI estimation platform built for their business.
description: A real Oxford, MS contractor running on a custom AI estimation platform built for their business.
fullDescription: MHP Construction is a licensed contractor in Oxford, Mississippi running the MsHomePros platform in production — the first real-world deployment of the ConstructionAI estimation engine. The work covers the full build: an AI estimator tuned to their pricing, professional proposal generation, and job tracking, delivered as a working tool a contractor actually uses to win and run jobs.
challenge: A working contractor needed to turn around accurate, professional estimates faster than pricing by hand — without learning a complicated piece of software.
solution: Deployed the MsHomePros platform with ConstructionAI behind it, tuned to their real pricing, so estimates and proposals go out in minutes instead of evenings.
features: Custom AI estimator tuned to their pricing
Professional proposals generated in minutes
Job tracking built around how they actually work
Live, in-production use by a licensed Oxford contractor
seoTitle: Case Study — AI Estimation for MHP Construction, Oxford MS
seoDescription: How Walt Burge built and deployed a custom AI estimation platform for MHP Construction, a licensed contractor in Oxford, Mississippi — live in production.

title: Compliant Patient Intake
summary: A Suboxone & psychiatric practice's online intake rebuilt HIPAA / 42 CFR Part 2 compliant — PHI leak killed, five clinical measures, secure staff portal.
description: A Suboxone & psychiatric practice's online intake rebuilt HIPAA / 42 CFR Part 2 compliant — PHI leak killed, five clinical measures, secure staff portal.
fullDescription: A working Suboxone and psychiatric practice in Oxford, Mississippi was collecting full patient intakes — including PHQ-9 depression scores and substance-use history — through a website form that emailed everything to a personal inbox with no BAA. That's a real HIPAA and 42 CFR Part 2 exposure, and it's the exact gap most healthcare websites have. The work: rebuild the intake path end to end so patients complete the same branded forms, but every byte of PHI lands on BAA-covered infrastructure instead of an email thread.
challenge: The practice's online intake was shipping protected health information — clinical screening scores and full intake PDFs — through a consumer email service to a personal inbox. No BAA anywhere on the path, on a form patients used every day.
solution: Rebuilt the intake to submit to a secure API on BAA-covered AWS infrastructure. Office notifications are PHI-free (an opaque intake ID only); staff read full records in an authenticated portal with per-user accounts. Self-scheduling hands off to the practice's patient portal, and the branded five-measure clinical intake the practice wanted stayed exactly as designed.
features: Five clinical measures: PHQ-9, GAD-7, MDQ, PCL-C, ASRS
PHI stored only on BAA-covered infrastructure
PHI-free staff notifications with opaque intake IDs
Authenticated staff portal with per-user accounts
Patient self-scheduling hand-off
seoTitle: Case Study — HIPAA-Compliant Patient Intake, Oxford MS
seoDescription: How Walt Burge rebuilt a behavioral-health practice's patient intake to be HIPAA and 42 CFR Part 2 compliant — killing a PHI leak and shipping a secure staff portal, in Oxford, MS.

title: North Mississippi Home Pros
summary: A licensed contractor's WordPress site rebuilt as a fast custom Next.js brand site — curated project gallery and estimate-request intake.
description: A licensed contractor's WordPress site rebuilt as a fast custom Next.js brand site — curated project gallery and estimate-request intake.
fullDescription: North Mississippi Home Pros is a licensed contractor in Oxford, Mississippi whose public web presence was a dated WordPress install — slow, template-bound, and hard to keep current. The work: rebuild the whole site as a custom Next.js build with a curated portfolio of their real projects across residential, commercial, and outdoor work, sharper brand and hero, and an estimate-request intake wired into their tooling. Delivered and staged — it replaces the WordPress site when the domain cuts over.
challenge: An aging WordPress site that didn't match the quality of the company's actual work — slow to load, locked to a template, and painful to update with new projects.
solution: Rebuilt WordPress as a custom Next.js site: new hero and brand treatment, a curated project gallery organized by residential, commercial, and outdoor work, and an estimate-request flow feeding their back office.
features: Full WordPress-to-Next.js rebuild
Curated project gallery — residential, commercial, outdoor
Estimate-request intake wired to their tooling
Fast static delivery
seoTitle: Case Study — Custom Next.js Rebuild for North Mississippi Home Pros, Oxford MS
seoDescription: How Walt Burge rebuilt North Mississippi Home Pros' WordPress site as a custom Next.js brand site with a curated project gallery and estimate-request intake — Oxford, MS.
